array_types.name: MEMPOWERDOWN_SRAM_Field_Array
array_types.code:
type MEMPOWERDOWN_SRAM_Field_Array is array (0 .. 5) of Boolean
  with Component_Size => 1, Size => 6;

record_types.name: DBGFORCE_Register

fields.name: PROC0_SWDO
fields.description: Directly drive processor 0 SWDIO input, if PROC0_ATTACH is set

fields.name: PROC0_SWDI
fields.description: Directly drive processor 0 SWCLK, if PROC0_ATTACH is set

fields.name: PROC0_SWCLK
fields.description:
Attach processor 0 debug port to syscfg controls, and disconnect it
from external SWD pads.

fields.name: PROC0_ATTACH
fields.description: Read-only. Observe the value of processor 1 SWDIO output.

fields.name: PROC1_SWDO
fields.description: Directly drive processor 1 SWDIO input, if PROC1_ATTACH is set

fields.name: PROC1_SWDI
fields.description: Directly drive processor 1 SWCLK, if PROC1_ATTACH is set

fields.name: PROC1_SWCLK
fields.description:
Attach processor 1 debug port to syscfg controls, and disconnect it
from external SWD pads.

fields.name: PROC1_ATTACH
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: MEMPOWERDOWN_Register

fields.name: SRAM
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: USB
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: ROM
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: MEMPOWERDOWN_SRAM_Field
PROC_CONFIG_Register

fields.name: PROC0_HALTED
fields.description: Read-only. Indication that proc1 has halted

fields.name: PROC1_HALTED
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_2_23
fields.description:
Configure proc0 DAP instance ID.\n Recommend that this is NOT changed
until you require debug access in multi-chip environment\n WARNING:
do not set to 15 as this is reserved for RescueDP

fields.name: PROC0_DAP_INSTID
fields.description:
Configure proc1 DAP instance ID.\n Recommend that this is NOT changed
until you require debug access in multi-chip environment\n WARNING:
do not set to 15 as this is reserved for RescueDP

record_types.name: PROC_IN_SYNC_BYPASS_HI_Register

fields.name: PROC_IN_SYNC_BYPASS_HI
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: PROC_IN_SYNC_BYPASS_Register

fields.name: PROC_IN_SYNC_BYPASS
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: SYSCFG_Peripheral

fields.name: PROC0_NMI_MASK
fields.description:
Processor core 1 NMI source mask\n Set a bit high to enable NMI from
that IRQ

fields.name: PROC1_NMI_MASK
fields.description: Configuration for processors

fields.name: PROC_CONFIG
fields.description:
For each bit, if 1, bypass the input synchronizer between that GPIO\n
and the GPIO input register in the SIO. The input synchronizers
should\n generally be unbypassed, to avoid injecting metastabilities
into processors.\n If you're feeling brave, you can bypass to save
two cycles of input\n latency. This register applies to GPIO 0...29.

fields.name: PROC_IN_SYNC_BYPASS
fields.description:
For each bit, if 1, bypass the input synchronizer between that GPIO\n
and the GPIO input register in the SIO. The input synchronizers
should\n generally be unbypassed, to avoid injecting metastabilities
into processors.\n If you're feeling brave, you can bypass to save
two cycles of input\n latency. This register applies to GPIO 30...35
(the QSPI IOs).

fields.name: PROC_IN_SYNC_BYPASS_HI
fields.description: Directly control the SWD debug port of either processor

fields.name: DBGFORCE
fields.description:
Control power downs to memories. Set high to power down memories.\n
Use with extreme caution

variables.name: SYSCFG_Periph
variables.code:
SYSCFG_Periph : aliased SYSCFG_Peripheral
  with Import, Address => SYSCFG_Base;
